probe: BURST arc completion: verify-stream (gemma4_verify_t_am_stream, per-layer append_rows_dc + counter-based attention arms) + M-round burst loop on round_stream generics (one host sync per M rounds)
verdict: CORRECTNESS LOCKED: stream 128/128 on 26B+31B, short+depth, M=1..4. Three parity bugs found+fixed on the way: (1) eager hd256 verify rows ran rows_v4 while stream ran rows_v4_dc - unified both on the _dc symbol (arg-store counter), eager unchanged (0.938/128-128); (2) fa_decode_dc's hd512-under-floor arm was believed mis-mirrored (vec_q probe 0/128 disproved that - eager IS scalar under the floor); (3) THE REAL FLIPPER: dc scalar partitioned keys by ceil(bucket/sk) while eager partitions ceil(t_kv/sk) - different FP order whenever the ceilings differ, deterministic 31B verify argmax flips (50/128). Fix = ONE nullable-ctr fa_decode_f32 symbol computing ns_eff=ceil(T_kv/split_keys) in-kernel (grid stays bucket-sized, dead splits write NEG_INF partials); f32_dc twin deleted. EVERIFY_DC exposure probe 50/128 -> 128/128 after. Battery: kernel-check 27B ALL GREEN, 9B argmax MATCH + run-spec K1-8 PASS, 31B eager floor2 87.6 (standing 88.0, no regression).
perf: burst M=4 single-stream is PERF-NEGATIVE everywhere (26B short 304-310 vs eager 379; 26B depth 199 vs 255; 31B short 74 vs 88): one stream = zero draft/verify overlap, so the burst only removes launch tax (already hidden at 96.7% busy) and pays for it with fixed-K drafting (adaptive kc is host logic). Default OFF (BW24_GEMMA_SPEC_BURST=0). The prize needs stage 2: second-stream SPECULATIVE draft(N+1) under verify(N) with discard-on-partial-accept; expected win bounded by full-accept-prob x drafter-share (~9% on 26B accept .93^6, ~2% on 31B .76^7 - K-shortening raises it).
config: BW24_GEMMA_SPEC_BURST=M (default 0) + requires BW24_GEMMA_DRAFT_GRAPH=1; burst gates: no pmin, no kv-shared tail, horizon on one side of win/fa512/rows-floor, ctx+gen headroom; BW24_BURST_VCHECK=1 = stream-verify-vs-eager in-place gate harness (kept, diagnostics class)
